market_summary: S&P 500 closed at 6,506 (-1.5%), down 7% from January all-time high. VIX at 26.78 reflects elevated fear from Middle East conflict escalation and hawkish Fed. Fourth consecutive weekly loss — longest losing streak in over a year.
key_drivers: Middle East conflict: Iran war escalating, Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, energy supply disruption
Fed hawkish: rates held at 3.50-3.75%, inflation forecast raised to 2.7%, no cuts expected this year
Triple witching added extra volatility to an already fragile market
Super Micro/Nvidia chip diversion charges weighed on semiconductor sector

trades_review.notes: Codex seed run picked MU as best opportunity with 0.72 confidence
Codex flagged PANW as sell with 0.74 confidence — cybersecurity may be risk-off beneficiary though

confidence_calibration.assessment: Day 1 — no calibration data yet. Codex ran first analysis via gpt-4o. Regime correctly identified as risk_off.

forward_outlook: Watch for: (1) Middle East conflict developments — any de-escalation = sharp rally, (2) Construction spending and PMI data next week, (3) Bond yields — 10Y above 4.5% adds more pressure on equities. Energy remains the safe haven play but is crowded.
lessons: Day 1: system bootstrapped successfully with gpt-4o via Codex CLI
Risk-off regime confirmed by VIX > 25 and defensive sector leadership
First seed run produced reasonable analysis — MU buy signal and PANW sell signal both have technical backing
